howItWorks.intro: This lumber cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Board Feet = (Thickness × Width × Length in inches) ÷ 144
Total Board Feet = BF per Board × Number of Boards
Total Cost = Total Board Feet × Price per Board Foot
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a board foot?
faq.answer: A board foot (BF) is 144 cubic inches of wood — equivalent to a board 1 inch thick × 12 inches wide × 12 inches long. Hardwood lumber is priced per board foot.

faq.question: How much does hardwood lumber cost?
faq.answer: Common hardwoods: Poplar $3-5/BF, Red Oak $5-8/BF, Cherry $7-10/BF, Walnut $10-15/BF, Maple $5-8/BF. Exotic woods can exceed $20-50/BF.

faq.question: How do I calculate board feet?
faq.answer: Board Feet = (Thickness × Width × Length) ÷ 144, where all measurements are in inches. A 1" × 6" × 8' (96") board = 4 board feet.

faq.question: Is the Lumber Cost Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Lumber Cost Calculator?
sections.body: The Lumber Cost Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your thickness, width, length, you get instant results including board feet, total board feet, total cost. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting board feet right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your thickness and need to find the right board feet. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Thickness (inches)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your thickness — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: width, length, number of boards, price per board foot. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your board feet, total board feet, total cost. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your thickness before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both board feet and total board feet — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your price per board foot, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
